$comment: ADOPTED SPEC (ADR-0059) — the TranslucentTB taskbar-translucency MODEL, distilled from TranslucentTB (github.com/TranslucentTB/TranslucentTB, settings.schema.json: $defs/TaskbarAppearance) into a byte-pinned, content-addressed κ-object. Hologram OS does NOT run TranslucentTB's C++/Win32 binary (ADR-0029: never run a foreign runtime); it adopts its accent-state model and expresses each state as the --holo-glass-* CSS tokens in holo-theme.css. holo-dock.css selects a state via #holo-dock[data-glass=...]. tools/holo-dock-witness.mjs proves this mapping is faithful.

accent.doc: The appearance mode applied to the bar.

color.doc: Tint color, #RRGGBBAA. The alpha is the tint-overlay opacity (0..255).

blur_radius.doc: Gaussian blur amount. TranslucentTB clamps to 750 and applies blur_radius/3; the CSS equivalent is backdrop-filter: blur(blur_radius/3 px).

normal.doc: System default — no effect. The bar reverts to no background.

clear.doc: Solid color WITH transparency; full alpha respected (ACCENT_ENABLE_TRANSPARENTGRADIENT).

acrylic.doc: Fluent acrylic material — blur + saturation boost + tint overlay (ACCENT_ENABLE_ACRYLICBLURBEHIND).

dynamic.doc: TranslucentTB swaps the accent state by context (desktop / visible-window / maximized / start-opened / search-opened / task-view / battery-saver). Hologram OS maps the two it can observe in-substrate.

rules.when: battery-saver OR device tier 'lean' OR reduced-motion
